name: loommark
description: A source-backed, live-rendering WYSIWYG Markdown editor for VS Code.

loommark.syncDelay.description: Milliseconds to group continuous input before updating the text document.

loommark.openByDefault.description: Open Markdown files with LoomMark by default. Disable this to use VS Code's normal editor association.

enumDescriptions: Follow the active VS Code color theme.
Use Crepe's warm editorial palette.
Use Crepe's neutral monochrome palette.
Use Crepe's cool Nord palette.
loommark.theme.description: Color theme used by the LoomMark editor. Light and dark variants follow VS Code automatically.

enumDescriptions: Render tables and edit cells in place. Enter or focus loss commits a cell, Escape cancels, Tab moves between cells.
Render tables as widgets and expand to Markdown source when the cursor enters the table.
loommark.table.description: How GFM tables are edited inside the LoomMark editor.

enumDescriptions: Bordered grid with lines around every cell.
Booktabs-style three-line table: heavy top and bottom rules, a header rule, and light row separators, with no vertical lines.
loommark.tableStyle.description: Visual style used when rendering GFM tables.

enumDescriptions: Hierarchical decimal numbering: 1, 2, 2.1, 2.2, 2.2.1.
Alternates arabic numerals, letters, and lowercase roman numerals per nesting level (1, a, i), then repeats.
loommark.orderedListStyle.description: How nested ordered list markers are numbered when rendered.

loommark.listGuides.description: Show connector lines between nested list items and their continuation content (indented paragraphs, quotes, code blocks). The line the cursor is on lights up its full ancestor chain.

enumDescriptions: No heading visualization.
A soft background color wash per heading section, no borders.
A colored left border bar per heading level, plus a faint background tint.
Each heading's section wrapped in a bordered, rounded card, nested for sub-headings.
loommark.cardMode.description: Visually distinguish which heading a line is under. Cycle through modes from the editor title bar.

loommark.cardBackgroundColors.description: Colors to cycle through per heading level for the Card background fill (CSS color values, e.g. "#ff8800"). Set to an empty array to draw no background at all.

loommark.cardBorderColors.description: Colors to cycle through per heading level for Card/accent borders and rails (CSS color values, e.g. "#ff8800"). Set to an empty array to draw no border at all.

loommark.cardBackgroundStrength.description: Amount of each heading accent mixed into its translucent Card surface.

loommark.cardBorderStrength.description: Strength of heading accent colors in Card borders. Lower values produce quieter, more neutral borders.

loommark.cardImage.enabled.description: Show a stable, independently selected image inside each heading section. Applies to the card and tint loommark.cardMode styles; has no effect for accent or off.

loommark.cardImage.path.description: Card image file, directory, or https:// URL. Leave empty to reuse loommark.background.path.

loommark.cardImage.opacity.description: Opacity of images drawn inside heading Cards.

loommark.cardImage.blur.description: Blur radius of Card images in pixels.

loommark.cardImage.overlay.description: Editor-theme overlay above Card images. Higher values improve text readability.

loommark.background.enabled.description: Show a configurable image behind the LoomMark editor.

loommark.background.path.description: Image file, directory, https:// URL, or //server/share/folder. Windows paths may use C:\\Pictures\\LoomMark.

enumDescriptions: Use the configured file, or the first image in a configured directory.
Choose a random image whenever the editor opens.
Choose one stable image per calendar day.
Choose a stable image based on the Markdown document path.
loommark.background.selection.description: How an image is selected when the configured path is a directory.

loommark.background.blur.description: Background image blur radius in pixels.

loommark.background.overlay.description: Opacity of the editor-theme color overlay above the image. Higher values improve text readability.

loommark.keyboardEditing.description: Allow the text cursor to enter rendered images, tables, and math with the keyboard so they can be edited without a mouse. When disabled, the cursor skips over them and editing starts on click.

enumDescriptions: Show both the in-editor outline and the Explorer view.
Show only the in-editor outline.
Show only the native Explorer view.
Disable both outlines.
loommark.outline.description: Controls where the Markdown document outline is shown.

scripts.vscode:prepublish: npm run build && npm run licenses
scripts.package: npm run clean && npm run check && npm run build && npm run licenses && vsce package --no-dependencies --out artifacts/loommark.vsix
